what_it_does: Records a noticeboard reply/note that argues iOS standalone PWA mode is a hard requirement for edge gestures to function and proposes a visual prompt telling users to add the app to their home screen.
observations: It is a comment/reply linked to item-5c2d936d via a commented_on relation.
Authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel.
Explains that Safari's bottom toolbar and edge-swipe-back/pull-to-refresh gestures conflict with the intended thumb gestures.
States that an installed standalone PWA removes the bottom bar and disables Safari edge-swipe-back.
Mentions theme-color, display: standalone, and apple-mobile-web-app-* meta tags as the mechanism.
Asks whether the app can detect a normal Safari tab versus a home-screen PWA.
Proposes a visual message or notification with screenshots showing how to save to the home screen.
Notes the app is currently single-user but expected to be used by others later.
standouts: Frames standalone PWA mode as a functional input requirement, not a cosmetic preference.
Identifies the Safari edge-swipe-back conflict as a device-only verification item.
Proposes concrete onboarding guidance with screenshots for future users.
concerns: Leaves the detection question open without a recorded answer.
Mixes a technical constraint and a UX proposal without a clear decision or next step.
Mentions providing screenshots but no attachments or location are recorded.
notes: Tim's note clarifying that standalone PWA mode is required for iOS edge gestures and suggesting a visual add-to-home-screen prompt with screenshots.
questions: Is there a reliable way to detect a normal Safari tab versus a standalone home-screen PWA?
What is the exact gesture set that conflicts with Safari chrome?
Which devices and iOS/Safari versions must be verified for the edge-swipe conflict?
Where will the proposed screenshots be stored or referenced?
Does 'still have to work in Safari' mean full functionality or a degraded fallback?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to post a point/comment, and the content matches that as a discussion note combining technical rationale with a UX proposal.
apparent_themes: PWA installability versus browser chrome conflicts
Scaling from single-user to multi-user experience
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to secure the technical feasibility of a gesture feature on iOS by establishing standalone PWA mode as a requirement, while also planning user-facing guidance so future users know how to install the app.
novelty: Treats PWA standalone mode as a functional input requirement rather than a visual or branding preference.
contribution: Captures the Safari edge-gesture conflict and the idea of a visual add-to-home-screen prompt, which a fused system would keep as both a technical requirement and a UX onboarding consideration.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim replying to item-5c2d936d, arguing that iOS standalone PWA mode is required for edge gestures to work and proposing a visual prompt with screenshots to instruct users to add the app to their home screen.
intention_for_embedding: To record the rationale that PWA standalone mode is a hard requirement for gesture input and to propose onboarding guidance for future users.
